fields.description:
## Why
Offline FP/remediation analysis without taxing rebar's hot paths.

## What
- A reducer-IGNORED REVIEW_RESULT event capturing per-criterion verdicts + finding fingerprints + metadata +
  override justifications; the reducer skips the type; offline replay+join yields per-criterion fire/FP/FN rates.

- CALLER-VISIBLE FINDING ID: the stable per-finding `id` is MINTED by the orchestrator (`ca03`) at result assembly (the content fingerprint, sole owner); the sidecar RECORDS that same id/fingerprint and the Pass-4 coach (`75a9` crusty-herb-stand) references it (reference-not-restate) — one owner, no circular alignment, so the caller-facing reference and the offline-analysis record line up.
## Scope
- Capture only; analysis is offline; no human-feedback requirement.
- RETENTION/CLEANUP (T9/T5b): bound sidecar growth WITHOUT interfering with passive data-gathering — an
  age/size-based compaction or archival-to-analytics path that PRESERVES enough recent history for offline
  FP/FN analysis; reuse rebar's existing event compaction. The sidecar is intentionally APPEND-ONLY per
  review run (every run recorded for analysis — NOT idempotent, by design); idempotency lives on the
  ATTESTATION (head-state): re-running a review yields ONE current valid signature, not duplicates.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] REVIEW_RESULT sidecar emitted with finding fingerprints; the reducer skips the type.
- [ ] head / .cache / deps / validate are unaffected by sidecar size/count.
- [ ] Offline reconstruction of per-criterion FP/remediation is possible.
- [ ] A retention/cleanup path bounds sidecar growth while preserving recent history for offline analysis (compaction/archival; passive gathering uninterrupted).

## Acceptance Criteria (added — close G3 coverage)
- [ ] Capture per-pass LATENCY + COST (the fast claim-path check and the out-of-band review) in the sidecar metadata, so latency/cost targets set initially from UX/timeout projections are refined PASSIVELY from dogfood data (no upfront benchmark step).
- [ ] ROLLOUT / cross-clone coexistence: the new `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar + the attestation head-state event follow rebar's preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones pattern — an older clone predating the feature PRESERVES-and-IGNORES the event (never mis-reduces it), `fsck` WARNs on event types newer than the running binary, and reconcile hosts upgrade first (readers-before-writers).
